reasoning:
The US reported a robust +3.0% advance estimate for Q2 2025 real GDP on July 30th, 2025. Prior to this, Q1 2025 had printed a -0.5% (3rd estimate), which had stoked recession fears and drove earlier market probabilities up to ~19%. By printing +3.0% for Q2, the possibility of Q1 and Q2 forming two consecutive negative quarters is effectively closed (an advance estimate revision of 3.0 percentage points downward to negative territory in the final 3rd estimate is historically negligible). 

As a result, for this market to resolve to YES, a new sequence must begin before the end of 2025. Specifically, this requires either (Q3 2025 and Q4 2025) or (Q4 2025 and Q1 2026) to be negative. Both combinations strictly require Q4 2025 to be negative. Given the economy's renewed strength (+3.0% growth in Q2), the baseline probability of an impending sudden two-quarter contraction starting in the next two quarters is quite low, approximating the base rate of 5-8%. The prediction market odds for a 2025 recession had already tumbled to ~18% before the Q2 numbers came in as strong as they did, and the resolution criteria now demand an even stricter structural collapse in late 2025. I am highly confident in a low probability.
